title: ALIGN: Open-Source Analog Layout Automation from the Ground Up
abstract: We present ALIGN, an open-source, fully automated analog layout tool that translates SPICE netlists to design-rule-correct GDS layouts. ALIGN encodes layout symmetry, device pairing, common-centroid placement, and shielding constraints extracted directly from the netlist topology.

title: MAGICAL: Toward Fully Automated Analog IC Layout Leveraging Human and Machine Intelligence
abstract: MAGICAL is a fully automated analog IC layout system that combines human design knowledge encoded as layout constraints with machine intelligence for placement and routing. The system generates DRC-clean layouts from SPICE netlists targeting advanced FinFET processes.

title: AutoCkt: Deep Reinforcement Learning of Analog Circuit Designs
abstract: AutoCkt applies deep reinforcement learning to automate analog circuit sizing. A trained RL agent navigates the design space of transistor sizes and bias currents to meet user-specified performance targets, demonstrating transfer learning across circuit topologies.

title: GCN-RL Circuit Designer: Transferable Transistor Sizing with Graph Neural Networks and Reinforcement Learning
abstract: This paper introduces a learning-based framework for automatic transistor sizing using graph convolutional networks combined with reinforcement learning. By representing circuits as graphs, the model transfers knowledge across different technologies and circuit topologies to improve optimization efficiency.

title: PPAAS: PVT and Pareto Aware Analog Sizing via Goal-conditioned Reinforcement Learning
abstract: The paper proposes a goal-conditioned reinforcement learning framework for analog device sizing that generalizes across diverse design targets and PVT corners. Its main contributions are a Pareto-front-based goal sampling strategy, conservative hindsight replay, and a skip-on-fail simulation method, which together improve training and simulation efficiency.

title: Simultaneous Multi-Topology Multi-Objective Sizing  Across Thousands of Analog Circuit Topologies
abstract: MOJITO is a system for simultaneously optimizing thousands of analog circuit topologies to produce a diverse set of designs that trade off performance metrics. It represents the search space using hierarchical combinations of trusted analog building blocks and avoids manual rule specification by relying on SPICE-based evaluation. The system uses a multi-objective evolutionary algorithm with an age-layered population structure to balance exploration and exploitation, and it is demonstrated on 3,528 operational amplifier topologies.

title: AnalogGenie-Lite: Enhancing Scalability and Precision in Circuit Topology Discovery through Lightweight Graph Modeling
abstract: AnalogGenie-Lite is a decoder-only transformer for discovering analog circuit topologies using a lightweight graph representation. It introduces linear-complexity device-pin encoding, subgraph mining, and optimized sequence modeling, achieving significantly better scalability and validity than prior methods.

title: KCLNet: Electrically Equivalence-Oriented Graph Representation Learning for Analog Circuits
abstract: KCLNet is a graph representation learning framework for analog circuits that incorporates electrical priors, specifically Kirchhoff’s Current Law, to guide message passing in a DAG-based circuit representation. By enforcing current-conservation constraints through an asynchronous GNN and contrastive learning, it learns physically consistent circuit embeddings that improve performance on tasks like circuit classification, subcircuit detection, and graph edit distance prediction.

title: Differentiable Neural Network Surrogate Models for gm/ID-based Analog IC Sizing Optimization
abstract: This paper presents an improved optimization flow for analog integrated circuit sizing that integrates the gm/ID design methodology with machine learning–based surrogate models. It replaces expensive circuit simulations with neural networks that predict performance metrics, achieving ~3400× speedup and enabling gradient-based optimization methods like automatic differentiation. The approach is demonstrated on operational amplifier sizing and supports better reuse of designs across technology nodes.
